headline: Too Many Thunderstorms?
subtext: The frequency of thunderstorms in the area has increased to an alarming rate, causing havoc and disrupting daily life. Ratepayers are up in arms as their gardens, homes, and businesses are wrecked by these storms. The Council is under immense pressure to provide effective solutions before the situation gets out of hand.
body:
In an unprecedented move by the local council, Councillor (Cr) Penelope Pricklebottom has proposed that residents of Northern Beaches should start carrying umbrellas, as thunderstorms have been happening more frequently than not. "It's all about consistency," Cr Pricklebottom told The Remy Digest in a recent interview at the Council Chambers.

The proposal suggests that citizens should adapt to this new reality and embrace their British roots by carrying brollies (umbrellas) around with them wherever they go, including while walking along the footpath that runs parallel to The Steyne. "It's not like we have any say in the matter," said a local ratepayer, who wished to remain anonymous for fear of reprisals from Cr Pricklebottom.

The idea has divided the community. Some residents believe that it is high time they start taking the weather conditions on their beloved beachside home turf seriously and embrace their inner Britishness. However, others argue that umbrellas are an unnecessary encumbrance and do little to help with the growing issue of rubbish (garbage) piling up outside the Council Chambers.

The situation has escalated, as a B-Line bus driver reported seeing a group of young beach-goers walking along The Corso clad in full rain gear. "It's just not right," said the driver, shaking his head at the thought of all those plastic ponchos and brightly coloured brollies littering the footpath after a particularly wild thunderstorm.

The Remy Digest reached out to Professor Pluvius Piddlebottom, a weather expert from The University of Wet 'n' Wild. When asked about Cr Pricklebottom's proposal that residents should carry umbrellas due to excessive thunderstorms, Prof. Piddlebottom quipped: "I'd suggest they bring a boat as well."

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.

classifieds: For Sale: Haunted Teapot. Spills when you're not looking. $50
Lost & Found: Pet Sphinx. Disappeared on the night of a blue moon. No questions asked. Contact The Remy Digest.
Services: Professional Guilt Tripper. Specialize in extracting your money's worth from friends and family.

mystic.prediction: "Virgo of Northern Beaches, prepare for tedious paperwork and unrelenting scrutiny of minor details in your daily tasks. The Bin Chickens may poop on your path to prosperity; embrace it, as it's an opportunity to learn from messiness. The Mystic sees the real estate market fluctuating around The B-Line Bus route, so invest wisely or let go of those mundane beach houses."
